header.topic: Introduction to PowerPoint and Outline View Mode

learning_sequence_context.position_in_sequence: Lesson 4 of 10 in the Term 1-2 sequence - Lesson 2 of the 8-lesson PowerPoint unit.
learning_sequence_context.previous_lesson_summary: Last lesson pupils created an 'About Me' mind map with a central theme, main branches, and sub-branches.
learning_sequence_context.next_lesson_link: Next lesson pupils add copyright-free images to the slides they have just outlined.

expected_outcomes.lesson_objective: Pupils can describe why PowerPoint is used, open and save a new PowerPoint file, describe what outline view is and what it is used for, and can transfer the information from their 'About Me' mind map into outline view.
assessment_criteria: Each student will open a new PowerPoint file and save it to their OneDrive.
Each student will switch to outline view and explain what it is used for.
Each student will transfer at least three branches from their mind map into the outline as slide titles with bullet points.
expected_outcomes.activity_summary: Individual construction of a PowerPoint outline directly from each pupil's own 'About Me' mind map, using outline view to turn branches into slide titles and sub-branches into bullet points.

resources: Pupils' completed mind maps from Lesson 3

key_vocabulary.definition: A PowerPoint view that shows slide titles and bullet points as a structured text list, without slide design visible.
Microsoft's cloud storage service used to save and access files from any device.

preparation.additional_adult_role: Support pupils who cannot locate their saved mind map, using File Explorer/OneNote to help them find it before the outline task begins.
accessibility: Step-by-step printed instruction card for opening, saving, and switching to outline view

teaching_episode.teacher_actions: Ask pupils to open their saved mind map and identify, on mini-whiteboards, its central theme and how many main branches it has.
teaching_episode.pupil_actions: Open their mind map and note the central theme and branch count.

check_for_understanding.question: What is a mind map used for?
check_for_understanding.decision_rule: Proceed if most pupils can state 'planning'/'organising ideas'; otherwise briefly recap.

teaching_episode.teacher_actions: Explain why PowerPoint is used for presentations, and model opening a new PowerPoint file and saving it to OneDrive with a sensible file name.
teaching_episode.pupil_actions: Watch the demonstration and identify the Save and Save As options.

teaching_episode.teacher_actions: Model switching to outline view and transferring a sample mind map's branches into slide titles and bullet points, thinking aloud about how a main branch becomes a slide title and a sub-branch becomes a bullet.
teaching_episode.pupil_actions: Watch and question; identify verbally when a new slide title versus a new bullet point should be added.

check_for_understanding.question: Should this branch be a new slide title or a bullet point under the current slide?
check_for_understanding.decision_rule: Move to independent construction regardless; use responses to judge how much further modelling is needed.

teaching_episode.teacher_actions: Set the outline construction task using pupils' own mind maps; circulate offering least-help-first prompts to pupils who have stalled.
teaching_episode.pupil_actions: Open a new PowerPoint, save it to OneDrive, switch to outline view, and transfer their mind map's branches and sub-branches into slide titles and bullet points.

check_for_understanding.question: Have you got a slide title for each main branch of your mind map?
check_for_understanding.decision_rule: Pupils below three slide titles at the 15-minute mark receive a targeted prompt referencing their mind map.

teaching_episode.teacher_actions: Cold-call two pupils to share their outline under the visualiser; class checks that slide titles match main branches and bullets match sub-branches.
teaching_episode.pupil_actions: Compare their own outline against the shared examples and correct any mismatches.

check_for_understanding.question: Exit ticket: how many slides does your outline currently have?
check_for_understanding.decision_rule: Pupils with fewer than three slides are flagged for a brief catch-up prompt at the start of next lesson.
